npc_bios.aldric_vane: The guild master weighs every word against its profit margin before giving it credence. Long years of trade have made him slow to believe rumor — and quicker still to use it once he does.
npc_bios.sybil_oats: The heart of the tavern, Sybil knows every face that passes through her door and has a kind word for all of them. She keeps her loyalties as loose as her purse strings — warmth for everyone, allegiance for no one.
npc_bios.oswin_tanner: A solitary man who speaks little and listens much, Oswin spends his days at the tanning yard with only the smell of hides for company. He believes most of what he is told, for few bother to tell him anything at all.
npc_bios.marta_coin: Marta's stall is less a place of trade than a hub of chatter, where gossip flows as freely as coin. She has a talent for drawing strangers into conversation and remembering every word they say.
npc_bios.rufus_bolt: Rufus shapes metal and minds alike — blunt, direct, and hard to fool. He speaks plainly and takes pride in judging a man by his calluses, not his claims.
npc_bios.nell_picker: Quick to laugh and quick to believe, Nell moves through the tavern crowd like a fish in water. She carries a dozen half-remembered stories at any given moment and shares them all just as readily.
npc_bios.cob_farrow: Cob arrives trailing tales from distant roads, each story fresher than the last and none quite checkable. He has no fixed allegiance — only the next deal and the next horizon.
npc_bios.idris_kemp: Idris keeps the mill turning and the grain flowing without fuss or favor. He is neither fool nor schemer — a steady man in an unsteady world, content to do his work and leave the politics to others.
npc_bios.bess_wicker: Bess minds the stores with the cold efficiency of someone who trusts inventory over instinct. She speaks when spoken to, and almost never believes what she is told.
npc_bios.sim_carter: Sim hauls goods from the warehouse to wherever they are needed, picking up rumors along every route. He is quick to accept what he hears and just as quick to pass it along at the market.
npc_bios.greta_flint: Greta moves freely through the merchant quarter with a bright smile and an open ear. She takes nearly everything she hears at face value, and the town's gossip network would be much poorer without her.
npc_bios.edric_fenn: The alderman rules this town with measured restraint, bound by duty and all but immune to whisper or rumor. His loyalty to the established order is bone-deep — a harder man to deceive would be difficult to find.
npc_bios.isolde_fenn: Composed and watchful, Isolde balances her husband's severity with a gentler face to the town. She is well-liked and well-connected, and knows whose loyalty can be counted upon.
npc_bios.calder_fenn: Young and hot-blooded, Calder chafes against his father's rigid household and seeks his own reputation in the tavern and the street. He is easily stirred by talk of slights and glory alike.
npc_bios.bram_guard: The guard captain is a stoic man who serves the alderman with unflinching dedication. He regards most gossip with professional suspicion — but when he does act on information, he acts decisively.
npc_bios.wynn_gate: Wynn stands his post with a workman's reliability — not especially curious, not especially corrupt. He does his job, follows orders, and minds his own business most of the time.
npc_bios.pell_gate: Pell wears the alderman's colors but his heart belongs to whoever is buying the next round. He takes rumors at face value and has few real loyalties — a guard with wandering allegiances.
npc_bios.annit_scribe: Annit records the town's business in careful script and prefers the permanence of ledgers to the noise of conversation. She is meticulous, distant, and not easily drawn into the affairs of others.
npc_bios.tomas_reeve: Tomas arrives at the market with ledger and warrant in hand, unmoved by pleas or stories. He has heard every excuse and believes almost none of them — numbers, not words, are his currency.
npc_bios.old_hugh: Once the alderman's trusted man, Hugh now lives in the long shadow of retirement and memory. He is prone to believing old tales and new ones alike, and his loyalty to the current order has grown thin with age.
npc_bios.aldous_prior: The prior carries himself with the unhurried certainty of a man who answers to powers above this town's politics. He is not easily swayed by rumor, and his loyalty to his faith — and its earthly allies — is absolute.
npc_bios.maren_nun: Maren moves quietly through the sick and suffering with gentle hands and steady faith. Devoted to her order, she asks few questions and shares fewer secrets — but what she knows, she keeps close.
npc_bios.finn_monk: Young and restless beneath his habit, Finn is more likely to be found at the tavern than at vespers. He believes nearly everything he is told, and his vows sit somewhat loosely on his conscience.
npc_bios.vera_midwife: Vera tends births and fevers with knowledge that sits outside the chapel's blessing — half herbal, half habit, and all practical. She keeps one foot in folk tradition and one in careful observation.
npc_bios.old_piety: Old Piety has seen more seasons than most and believes more of what she hears, her faith leaving little room for skepticism. She is deeply attached to the church and its way of things, and slow to question either.
npc_bios.jude_bellringer: Jude rings the chapel bell and keeps the candles lit without much ceremony or complaint. He is an ordinary man in an ordinary role — neither especially devout nor especially doubtful.
npc_bios.constance_widow: Since her husband's passing, Constance has filled her days with chapel and market in equal measure. She is warm and talkative, quick to accept what she is told, and an eager conduit for the town's whispered news.
npc_bios.thomas_pilgrim: Thomas carries a pilgrim's staff and a restless soul, pausing here as he has paused in a dozen other towns. He is credulous of signs and wonders, and his loyalty to this place extends precisely as far as his next destination.
npc_bios.alys_herbwife: Alys keeps her own counsel and her own garden, trading in remedies the clergy tolerates but does not quite endorse. She is not unfriendly — merely apart, owing her allegiances to no house or faction.
npc_bios.denny_gravedigger: Denny spends his days among the dead and has little patience for the living's foolishness. He is quick to anger and slow to believe, a man hardened by his work into something neither sentimental nor easily deceived.

location_descriptions.manor: The alderman's residence and seat of local authority, its stone walls and heavy gate a quiet reminder of who holds power in this town.
location_descriptions.tavern: The warm heart of the town's social life, where news, drink, and dubious stories all flow in equal measure.
location_descriptions.chapel: A modest but earnest place of worship where the town's faithful gather — and where whispers sometimes carry further than prayers.
location_descriptions.market: The busy open square where merchants compete for coin and gossip, and where the town's news is traded as freely as any goods.
location_descriptions.blacksmith: The ringing of Rufus's hammer sets the rhythm of the working day; a place of heat and iron where practical men come on practical business.
location_descriptions.mill: The mill's wheel turns without pause, grinding grain and feeding half the town, its steady sound a constant backdrop to daily life.
location_descriptions.trader_stall: A cluster of open stalls where the guild's merchants hawk their wares beneath weathered awnings and watchful eyes.
location_descriptions.storage: The warehouse district holds the town's surplus in cool shadow — a practical place, largely overlooked except by those who need it.
location_descriptions.tanner: The tanning yard sits at the edge of town for good reason; the smell of cured hides carries, and Oswin prefers the solitude that drives most others away.
location_descriptions.town_hall: The civic hall where the alderman conducts official business, its doors open to those with legitimate grievances and firmly shut to those without.
location_descriptions.guardpost: A sturdy watchtower near the town square where the guard keeps vigil, its narrow windows offering a clear view of the comings and goings below.
location_descriptions.graveyard: A quiet and well-kept plot beyond the chapel where the town's dead rest, tended by Denny's steady if grim attention.
location_descriptions.well: The town well draws a steady trickle of townsfolk at all hours — a natural crossing-point for news, greetings, and the occasional overheard word.
